title: The Last Hunt
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: An aging Comanche tracker is hired by the Texas Ranger who once hunted his people to find a kidnapped girl in the harsh Texas plains.
long_description:
Santiago has spent forty years living quietly on the edge of settlement, carrying the scars of the Reservation and the loss of his family. When Ranger McAllister — the same man who once hunted his people during the Indian Wars — arrives at his door asking for help finding a kidnapped girl, Santiago agrees not for the Ranger, but for the girl.

What follows is a journey across the unforgiving Texas landscape that forces both men to reckon with the violence that shaped them. As the trail leads deeper into Comanche country, old debts surface and old wounds reopen. The girl, Maria Gutierrez, is no passive victim — she watches, she waits, and she plans.

The Last Hunt is a spare, literary Western about memory, justice, and the fragile possibility of redemption between enemies.
main_characters: Santiago — Comanche elder and tracker, nearly seventy, carries the weight of his people's loss
Ranger Elias McAllister — Texas Ranger, former Indian fighter, struggling with the ghosts of his past
Maria Gutierrez — Fifteen-year-old kidnapped girl, fierce and resourceful, refuses to be a victim
Tomas Gutierrez — Maria's father, tenant farmer, hollowed by grief
